title: Reslian Purge

{{Quote|Those causing the Reslian Purge believed someday a Man-Hutt would sweep evil from the Galaxy.|Soontir Fel|<ref name="RS25" />}}
The '''Reslian Purge''' was the genocide of the inhabitants of several [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] worlds by the [[Lortan Fanatics]] approximately from [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]] to [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]].

==The Purge==
{{Quote|I've often wondered what would have happened to him if he hadn't begun his Imperial service flying against such obvious evil.|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] on Soontir Fel's involvement in the Reslian Purge|<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
In [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]],<ref name="UAA" /> expansionist [[Lortan]] fanatics who possessed a battle [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] swept across twelve [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] and destroyed everything in their path.<ref name="GG12" /> They believed that someday a Man-[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] would sweep evil from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="RS25" /> Among the Lortans' victims were [[Rrulinn]], [[Quaensan Prime]], the [[Dalisor Merchant Fleet]], and the [[Jiroch system/Legends|Jiroch system]], home of the [[Tunroth]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="GG12" />

[[File:Reslian_Purge_end.jpg|thumb|left|180px|[[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel's]] [[6/37th]] destroys the last [[Lortan]] base.]]
The Tunroth were primitive hunters and lacked the weaponry necessary to repel the Lortan invaders; millions of Tunroth were killed in what became known as the Reslian Purge,<ref name="UAA" /> with their population reduced to four million beings over a period of three months,<ref name="GG12" /> and most of the [[Tunroth Hunter]]s perished.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] eventually interceded in the conflict to safeguard Imperial interests,<ref name="UAA" /> with the intervention of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] preventing the Lortans from spreading their attacks further.<ref name="GG12" />

The Lortans were subsequently annihilated by the Empire. [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] played a prominent role in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the last battles of the conflict.<ref name="RS25" />

==Aftermath==
The loss of most of their population in the Reslian Purge was somewhat sobering to the Tunroth, and they were still in the process of coming to terms with their tragedy as late as [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. While most Tunroth realized that the Imperial effort to bring the Purge to an end was made primarily out of self-interest, the Tunroth were somewhat grateful to the Empire for coming to their aid<ref name="GG12" /> and reluctantly accepted Imperial rule, with many Tunroth becoming supporters of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Tunroth leaders who forbade [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] in favor of traditional hunting weapons changed their views after the Reslian Purge, and the Tunroth would later stockpile blasters and other modern weapons for the express purpose of defending their communes.<ref name="UAA" /> [[Kiran Tatch]] was one of the few Tunroth Hunters who survived the Purge, and it was believed that the trauma he experienced during the Purge transformed him a bitter being with little interest in anything other than the hunt.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
''X-wing Rogue Squadron 25: The Making of Baron Fel'' incorrectly spelled this as the "Resalian Purge," despite its name deriving from the planet [[Jiroch-Reslia]]. This misspelling also appears in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''.
